title: Per-stage progress comments on the source GitHub issue
description: S-006 comments on the source issue only once, at the terminal outcome (done/blocked) when it opens the PR. For the phone-facing taskboard the human wants to watch progress live. Add per-stage progress comments: as a GH-sourced ticket advances stages, post a concise comment on the issue so the phone gets a running log. Best-effort (never affects outcome), bounded (one comment per stage transition, not per iteration), and only for GH-sourced stories (S-NNN have no source issue). Implemented by injecting a progress callback into adw/orchestrator.py run_ticket, like verify_fn, so the orchestrator remains the only component that posts to GitHub; stage agents never call GitHub directly.
acceptance_criteria: For a GH-<n> story, the orchestrator posts a progress comment on each stage transition (stage started/finished with outcome); S-NNN stories post nothing
Comments are best-effort: any GitHub, credential, or offline failure is printed and swallowed, never changing the outcome or halting the run
Comment volume is bounded: at most one comment per stage transition (no per-iteration spam)
The S-006 terminal done/blocked comment and PR are preserved, not duplicated
The progress poster is injected into run_ticket; agents never call GitHub directly and the orchestrator stays the sole authority
Unit tests with a fake GitHub client cover: per-stage comment for a GH story, none for S-NNN, failures swallowed; full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
